MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CH2M HILL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Joe Alioto Veronese, Esq. of Law Offices of Joseph L. Alioto 225-3027 for pltfs & opposing parties; John Foust, Esq. of Jones Day 875-5868 for Lennar defts; Thomas F. Vandenburg, Esq. of Dongell Lawrence Finney LLP 213/943-6100 for deft Gordon N. Ball, Inc.; George Avery Otstott, Esq. & Josette D. Johnson, Esq. of Brydon Hugo & Parker 808-0300 for deft CH2M Hill.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Court adopts its tentative ruling as final, and also the Court adds that the Plaintiffs' oral Motion for Leave to Amend the complaint was denied. Plaintiff did not properly notice the motion prior to coming to Court for oral argument and did not file it with the Court as required under the CCP and local Rules.
DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO BOTH EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS AND EXPOSURE TO "OTHER" TOXINS. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO SPECIFICALLY DELINEATE IN ITS COMPLAINT THE SPECIFIC TOXIN ALLEGED TO HAVE CAUSED PLAINTIFFS' INJURIES. BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71, 80. PLAINTIFFS' ONLY REFER TO ASBESTOS DUST. FURTHER, THE DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE IN EXHIBIT 4, 6, 14A-J, ARE SUSTAINED ON THE BASIS OF LACK OF PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE AND HEARSAY. ALSO DEFENDANT BALL'S OBJECTION TO EXHIBITS 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 AND 18 ARE SUSTAINED AS IRRELEVANT GIVEN THE BOCKRATH DECISION.
AS TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BASED ON THE PUBLIC NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION, IT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE OF THE PRESENCE OF ASBESTOS LADEN DUST AND THAT THE LEVELS, IF ANY, WERE INJURIOUS TO HEALTH. THERE IS, THEREFORE, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO CAUSATION.
FINALLY, PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ALSO FAIL AND DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED DISREGARD OF HEALTH THREAT CREATED BY AIRBORNE ASBESTOS AND PLAINTIFFS' FEARS OF DEVELOPING CANCER AS A RESULT OF THE EXPOSURE. AS NOTED ABOVE, PLAINTIFFS' FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO "EXPOSURE" AND UNFOUNDED FEAR DOES NOT SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. POTTER V. FIRESTONE TIRE & RUBBER CO. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 965, 1004. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
